summary: P1 moved the LabUtopia POC from black or cluttered frames to three evaluator readback-visible frames. Static USD placement and nested DryingBox handle discovery are sane. Task-level hiding makes pick readable and place basically readable for PM diagnosis. open_door runtime physics now starts at the expected closed joint target, and the latest formal single-handle front-camera retake makes the DryingBox frame, door panel, and one orange handle/action point visible. Independent image-only QA rates the old-vs-current PM comparison PASS, with open_door PASS/WARN as diagnostic evidence only. render_validation_not_passed remains the release blocker, so task_render_accepted and official_baseline_evaluable stay false.

level1_pick.visual_reason: Task-level hiding leaves the target bottle clearly visible on the table. This is PM-readable diagnostic evidence, but formal visual/pixel QA has not yet been signed off.

level1_place.visual_reason: The beaker and yellow target platform are visible in the same evaluator frame. The relation is understandable, though a closer camera would improve final PM polish.

level1_open_door.visual_reason: The formal single-handle front-camera retake shows the DryingBox/cabinet frame, door panel, and one orange handle/action point in one evaluator readback frame. Independent image-only QA rates it PASS/WARN as PM-facing diagnostic evidence. It is not official task acceptance because diagnostics still report render_validation_not_passed.

open_door_root_cause_review.summary: GenManip task UID/articulation discovery is working; the instability is downstream of malformed DryingBox USD/PhysX topology.
findings: The composed runtime prim still has root scale 0.001 instead of a baked identity-scale articulation.
Multiple rigid links use the basename mesh, matching duplicate link-name warnings.
Some rigid links retain non-finite centerOfMass or invalid principalAxes.
At least one fixed-joint body target resolves to a collision prim without PhysicsRigidBodyAPI.
The button prismatic joint adds an extra DOF, so runtime reports RevoluteJoint and PrismaticJoint.
recommended_fix_sequence: Extend static validation to fail on non-identity articulation root scale, duplicate rigid-link basenames, non-finite COM, invalid principal axes, joint targets without rigid bodies, and unexpected extra DOFs.
Replace incremental over fixes with a sanitized DryingBox runtime asset: baked scale, identity root scale, unique rigid link names, required links/joints only, and stable handle placement.
Regenerate the LabUtopia overlay scene.usda and rerun open_door diagnostics.
Accept open_door only after finite joint positions, no duplicate-link warnings, stable runtime_sanity, and a visually plausible door/handle frame.

latest_resolution.strategy: sanitized DryingBox surrogate with fixed base and one aligned revolute door joint
latest_resolution.remaining_blocker: diagnostics still report render_validation_not_passed, so official task render acceptance and baseline evaluability remain blocked

baseline_evaluability_reviewer.primary_blocker: formal task render acceptance is not signed off
baseline_evaluability_reviewer.secondary_blocker: Lift2 composite assets and official runner execution are not closed

browser_visual_review.image_requests: static HTML parse found six report images and all six files exist; DOM dump contains required old/new/open_door/render_validation text
browser_visual_review.notes: Desktop and mobile long screenshots show old JPGs and new square evaluator PNGs, including the latest single-handle open_door eval PNG. Chromium emitted expected headless DBus warnings only. This display audit does not mean task visual acceptance.

allowed: All three Franka POC tasks now produce non-black evaluator camera2 readback frames.
Static USD readback shows task objects and the nested DryingBox handle in plausible workspace coordinates.
The old top-level duplicated handle payload is removed; the handle is represented as part of the DryingBox assembly.
Task-level hiding makes pick and place readable as PM-facing diagnostic frames.

The latest open_door runtime diagnostic has stable DryingBox joint positions, matches the expected closed target [0.0], only exposes RevoluteJoint, and shows the DryingBox frame, door panel, and one orange handle/action point in the same eval readback frame.
blocked: Any claim that open_door visual evidence is reliable for baseline evaluation.
Any claim that the current report display QA equals task visual acceptance.

next_steps: Run formal visual QA for pick/place and keep task_render_accepted=false until signed off.
Keep diagnostics claim_boundary as the release gate; do not upgrade claims from readback_visible alone.
Use the latest single-handle front-camera open_door frame as PM-facing diagnostic evidence only.
Refresh browser display QA and formal task visual QA; keep task_render_accepted=false until render_validation passes.
